title: OSI, TCP/IP, Encapsulation, and Traffic Evidence · Incident Repair Bay
summary: Trace a client request through host, switch, router, firewall, service, and return path using captures and diagrams. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a layered packet-flow diagnosis and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain the responsibilities and failure evidence of all seven OSI reference-model layers.
Map TCP/IP link, internet, transport, and application functions to OSI layers without treating either as packet reality.
Trace data, segments or datagrams, packets, frames, and bits through encapsulation, forwarding, and decapsulation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
Use a changed debug case derived from this module: network models flows. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Net Osi Layers trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain the responsibilities and failure evidence of all seven osi reference-model layers.

content.text: For Net Osi Layers, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Net Osi Layers, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe investigation headed "# Evidence: net-packet-capture-limits-1e20zz" with a concrete hypothesis, one read-only network command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Net Packet Capture Limits into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: net-packet-capture-limits-1e20zz
# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable probable cause]
# command: choose one safe read-only simulator command
# observation: [replace with the result that supports or rejects the hypothesis]

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Packet Capture Limits applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Interpret frame, packet, transport, and application capture fields while accounting for vantage point, encryption, offload, and missing traffic.
